title: eriogonum robustum
intro: Eriogonum robustum, also known as the robust buckwheat or robust Mormon tea, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Eriogonum robustum include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune system function, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing its leaves and stems in hot water, or by making a tincture or salve for topical application.
